P1: Size of your corpus file (in MB)
2.5637 MB

P1: Steps used for curating and preprocessing corpus
step-1: Collected IITJ raw documents from website pages + academic regulations + programme/course booklets.
step-2: Removed obvious boilerplate/navigation artifacts (home, previous, next, view all, etc.) and formatting noise.
step-3: Removed non-English dominant lines using ASCII-letter ratio filtering.
step-4: Normalized Unicode characters, lowercased full text.
step-5: Removed URLs, domain-like strings, long number strings, and non-text symbols.
step-6: Tokenized with regex (alphabetic tokens + apostrophe support), removed one-letter fragments.
step-7: Built cleaned one-document-per-line corpus and computed stats/frequencies.

P1: Top-10 words frequency-wise
and, 18957, of, 14692, the, 9009, to, 6607, lectures, 5485, in, 4882, for, 4586, engineering, 2627, learning, 2343, design, 2292

P1: Interesting analogy
Using CBOW: phd is close to {year, ph, dual, type, engg}; this captures academic-program relation around dual-degree and doctoral context.

P2: What worked better and why?
BiLSTM worked best overall on this run, because it achieved the highest novelty rate (0.998) and the lowest final training loss (1.6139), while keeping high diversity (0.984). Attention+RNN had the highest diversity (0.994) but lower novelty (0.948). Vanilla RNN was intermediate.

P2: Number of parameters and model size in vanilla RNN
Parameters: 27032
Model size: 0.1031 MB
